highlights.title: 2026 CNBC Disruptor 50 list: Why Anthropic was No. 1 in this year's rankings
highlights.description: Anthropic, posting explosive growth for its powerful AI systems that enterprises trust, has leapfrogged OpenAI on the CNBC Disruptor 50 list as the new No. 1.

highlights.title: Google Agrees to Create AI Cloud Firm With Blackstone
highlights.description: Alphabet’s Google agreed to create an artificial intelligence cloud business with Blackstone, aiming to compete with companies like CoreWeave in a burgeoning market. The project will rely on an initial $5 billion in equity capital from Blackstone, which will become the majority owner, according to a statement Monday. Bloomberg's Neil Campling explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Google, Blackstone to Create AI Cloud Firm With In-House Chips
highlights.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google agreed to create an artificial intelligence cloud business with Blackstone Inc., aiming to compete with companies like CoreWeave Inc. in a burgeoning market.

highlights.title: Anthropic and U.S. government to face off in DC court over blacklisting of AI company
highlights.description: Anthropic sued the Defense Department in March after the agency declared the artificial intelligence startup a supply chain risk.

highlights.title: Google I/O primer: Alphabet's AI showcase is its chance to wow Wall Street
highlights.description: After a 140% stock surge and a sweeping Wall Street reset, Google’s developer conference has to prove the AI story is more than a market narrative.

highlights.title: Blackstone to invest $5 billion in AI infrastructure venture with Google, powered by TPU chips
highlights.description: Blackstone is partnering with Google to launch a U.S.-based AI infrastructure company powered by Google’s TPU chips.

highlights.title: Google I/O 2026 Live Blog: All the Gemini and Smart Glasses Updates as They Happen
highlights.description: Follow our live coverage of Google’s annual developer keynote, where the company will announce updates to its Gemini suite of AI tools and more details about Android XR smart glasses.

highlights.title: VMware quietly debuts Arm hypervisor tech preview
highlights.description: Supports Nvidia Grace and Ampere processors

highlights.title: Barclays Says Robots May Offset 60% of China’s Population Slump
highlights.description: China’s deployment of humanoid robots could offset as much as 60% of its projected decline in the labor force by 2035, according to Barclays Plc, helping sustain the world’s largest industrial base as the population shrinks at a pace not seen in decades.

highlights.title: Moonshot AI to Overhaul Structure to Win Nod for Hong Kong IPO
highlights.description: Moonshot AI has informed its investors it will revamp its corporate structure to pave the way for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, aligning with Beijing’s tightened requirements for Chinese companies seeking an overseas debut.

ai.title: Dexterous Robotic Hand Maker Linkerbot Is Said to Consider Hong Kong IPO
ai.description: Linkerbot, a Chinese startup that specializes in making highly dexterous robotic hands, is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

ai.title: Meta is reportedly 'reassigning' 7,000 employees to AI-focused roles
ai.description: The company is transferring workers to four new organizations building AI tools and apps, according to NYT and Reuters.

ai.title: StanChart to Cut Thousands of Jobs in AI Push
ai.description: Standard Chartered said it plans to eliminate thousands of support roles over the next four years, joining the ranks of global lenders using artificial intelligence to trim headcount. “It’s not about cost cutting," Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters said at a press briefing in Hong Kong. "It's about having a structurally more productive environment that is fit for future." Bloomberg's Tom Metcalf reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Fujikura Shares Plunge After Three-Year Forecast Dents AI Hope
ai.description: Fujikura Ltd. shares fell as much as 17% after its three-year forecast fell short of frothy expectations about artificial intelligence’s appetite for high-density optical-fiber networks.

ai.title: Australia’s Top Pension Sees Agentic AI Disrupting Its Industry
ai.description: AustralianSuper, the country’s largest pension fund, said agentic artificial intelligence could transform how it serves its 3.5 million members, likening the technology’s potential impact to the disruption AI is driving across retail and consumer services.

ai.title: Xiaomi’s upcoming YU7 GT just beat every SUV ever around Nürburgring
ai.description:
Xiaomi, a company that makes smartphones and other consumer electronics, sent its second EV around the famously difficult Nürburgring racetrack, and once again set a record with it, beating every other SUV, gas or electric.
more…

ai.title: Fake Samsung SSD spotting comes to CrystalDiskInfo as AI crunch drives sophisticated counterfeit market — free open-source software can flag clones by checking firmware, PCI Vendor ID
ai.description: CrystalDiskInfo's new feature will tell you whether your Samsung SSD is the real deal or not.

chips_cloud.title: Baidu says the quiet part out loud – you can’t build AI infrastructure, so clouds can cash in
chips_cloud.description: CFO says GPU rentals are ‘structurally higher margin than CPU cloud’

chips_cloud.title: Crypto Crime Escalates With Kidnappings, Cons and Human Coercion
chips_cloud.description: After a year of kidnappings, assaults and armed home invasions targeting cryptocurrency holders, the industry is racing to harden its defenses.

robotics.title: Barclays Says Robots May Offset 60% of China’s Population Slump
robotics.description: China’s deployment of humanoid robots could offset as much as 60% of its projected decline in the labor force by 2035, according to Barclays Plc, helping sustain the world’s largest industrial base as the population shrinks at a pace not seen in decades.

robotics.title: BNP Paribas' Zhang on Investing in Physical AI
robotics.description: Joy Zhang, APAC Capital Goods Analyst at BNP Paribas, says physical AI will be the ultimate application of the technology, paving the way for humanoid robots to enter daily life. She also discusses winners and losers in the space in an interview on Bloomberg’s The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)
